text.title: Golden-Master Corpus for AI-Driven Legacy Rewrites Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules grounding the test-golden-master-legacy-rewrite methodology plus the `skip-this-methodology` safety rule. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: The (input, expected_output) corpus MUST be committed in the repo (compressed if large, LFS-tracked if >100 MB). Generation scripts MUST be re-runnable from CI.
rule.rationale: An uncommitted corpus is no corpus; it cannot gate a rewrite or be diffed when the agent's output drifts.

rule.statement: The default pass criterion is byte-for-byte equality of `actual_output` and `expected_output`. Non-determinism must be eliminated by canonicalization (sort, redact, normalize) before comparison.
rule.rationale: Soft equality (substring, fuzzy) lets the AI hallucinate `close enough` output that diverges in production.

rule.statement: When a diff is intentional (bug fix, behaviour upgrade), it MUST be approved row-by-row in a structured review file (`approved_diffs.yaml`). Approving the whole corpus diff in one click is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Bulk approval erases the audit trail that distinguishes an intentional behaviour change from an accidental regression.

rule.statement: Inputs MUST be sampled to cover the legacy's real-world distribution (top-N by frequency + tail samples + adversarial fuzz). Random uniform sampling alone is insufficient.
rule.rationale: Real-world bugs hide in low-frequency edge cases; uniform sampling misses them.

rule.statement: Any non-deterministic output (timestamps, UUIDs, hash maps) MUST be canonicalized with a documented function before storage AND comparison. The canonicalization function is version-pinned alongside the corpus.
rule.rationale: Naive comparison of timestamps explodes the corpus into flaky red builds the first time the system clock moves.

rule.statement: Skip this methodology when the preconditions in `## Applies If` are not all true, or any item in `## Skip If` holds. The decision tree routes to this rule on those branches.
rule.rationale: A methodology that runs on the wrong input wastes tokens and produces misleading artifacts. The explicit skip rule is the safety valve.
